perturbation.original_text: (d) As used in this Agreement, the term "Post-Term Period" shall mean a continuous uninterrupted period of two (2) years from the date of: (a) a transfer permitted under Section 14 of the Franchise Agreement; (b) expiration or termination of the Franchise Agreement (regardless of the cause for termination); (c) termination of Member's employment with Franchisee; and/or (d) a final order of a duly authorized arbitrator, panel of arbitrators, or a court of competent jurisdiction (after all appeals have been taken) with respect to any of the foregoing or with respect to the enforcement of this Agreement; either directly or indirectly (through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any persons, partnership, corporation or entity).
perturbation.changed_text: (d) As used in this Agreement, the term "Post-Term Period" shall mean a continuous uninterrupted period of two (2) years from the date of: (a) a transfer permitted under Section 14 of the Franchise Agreement; (b) expiration or termination of the Franchise Agreement for cause attributable to the Franchisee; (c) termination of Member's employment with Franchisee; and/or (d) a final order of a duly authorized arbitrator, panel of arbitrators, or a court of competent jurisdiction (after all appeals have been taken) with respect to any of the foregoing or with respect to the enforcement of this Agreement; either directly or indirectly (through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any persons, partnership, corporation or entity). Notwithstanding the foregoing, in cases of termination of the Franchise Agreement without cause, the Post-Term Period shall be one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a condition to the definition of the "Post-Term Period." While the original text defines it as two years from the termination date regardless of the cause, the modified text specifies that if the termination is without cause, the Post-Term Period is reduced to one year. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the definition which stated the period is two years regardless of termination cause, creating uncertainty and potentially leading to disputes over the length of the non-compete obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Covenants Not to Compete. (a) Member specifically acknowledges that, pursuant to the Franchise Agreement, and by virtue of its position with Franchisee, Member will receive valuable specialized training and Confidential Information, including, without limitation, information regarding the operational, sales, promotional, and marketing methods and techniques of Franchisor and the System. (b) Member covenants and agrees that during the term of the Franchise Agreement, except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, for itself, or through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any person, persons, partnership, corporation, or entity: (i) Own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, rent or lease to, or have any other interest in business that (directly or indirectly) operates, or grants franchises or licenses to operate, a restaurant featuring pizza and related food specialties or that offers products or services substantially similar to those then offered by Pizza Fusions Restaurants ("Competitive Business"); C - 1 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015 (ii) Divert or attempt to divert any business or customer, or potential business or customer, to any Competitive Business; or (iii) Induce any person to leave his or her employment with Franchisee or Franchisor. (c) Member covenants and agrees that during the Post-Term Period (defined below), except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, or have any other interest in any Competitive Business that is, or intends to operate, within a three (3) mile radius of the premises of your Franchised Business or within a three (3) mile radius of any Franchised Business then-operating or under construction to operate under the System. (d) As used in this Agreement, the term "Post-Term Period" shall mean a continuous uninterrupted period of two (2) years from the date of: (a) a transfer permitted under Section 14 of the Franchise Agreement; (b) expiration or termination of the Franchise Agreement for cause attributable to the Franchisee; (c) termination of Member's employment with Franchisee; and/or (d) a final order of a duly authorized arbitrator, panel of arbitrators, or a court of competent jurisdiction (after all appeals have been taken) with respect to any of the foregoing or with respect to the enforcement of this Agreement; either directly or indirectly (through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any persons, partnership, corporation or entity). Notwithstanding the foregoing, in cases of termination of the Franchise Agreement without cause, the Post-Term Period shall be one (1) year.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the 'Post-Term Period' as a continuous two-year period from the date of termination of the Franchise Agreement regardless of the cause. The changed text introduces a condition that if the termination is without cause, the 'Post-Term Period' is one year. This directly contradicts the original definition and the first part of the modified definition in Section 2(d), creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Third-Party Beneficiary. Member hereby acknowledges and agrees that Franchisor is an intended third-party beneficiary of this Agreement with the right to enforce it, independently or jointly with Franchisee.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Third-Party Beneficiary. Member hereby acknowledges and agrees that Franchisor is an *potential* third-party beneficiary of this Agreement with the right to enforce it, *only if the Franchisee consents* independently or jointly with Franchisee.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the Franchisor is an intended third-party beneficiary with the right to enforce the agreement. The modified text introduces ambiguity by saying Franchisor is a 'potential' beneficiary, contradicting the original intent. The addition of 'only if the Franchisee consents' further diminishes the Franchisor's rights, making it contingent on the Franchisee's approval, hence an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Third-Party Beneficiary. Member hereby acknowledges and agrees that Franchisor is an *potential* third-party beneficiary of this Agreement with the right to enforce it, *only if the Franchisee consents* independently or jointly with Franchisee.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Franchisor 'is an intended third-party beneficiary', granting them specific rights. The changed text modifies this to a 'potential' beneficiary, contingent on the Franchisee's consent. This creates a direct contradiction as it introduces ambiguity and dependence where there was previously a clear entitlement, aligning with the definition of inconsistencies causing an in-text contradiction. The contradiction is located within the same section, reinforcing the internal inconsistency.
